title: Portfolio Management Techniques: A portfolio manager is evaluating two stocks. Stock A has...
summary: Calculate portfolio expected return as the weighted average of component asset returns.
prompt: A portfolio manager is evaluating two stocks. Stock A has an expected return of 12% and a standard deviation of 20%, while Stock B has an expected return of 8% and a standard deviation of 10%. If the correlation coefficient between A and B is 0.1, what is the expected return on the minimum-variance portfolio?

options.text: The expected return on the minimum-variance portfolio is calculated using the
Minimizes risk for a given return
Calculated using weights and standard deviations

solution: The expected return on the minimum-variance portfolio is calculated using the weights of each stock based on their standard deviations and correlation.
